assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All 4 slices (1,024 TPU chips total) will be recreated simultaneously — not rolling' and contrasts this with CPU/GPU node upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'A maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment' and 'Even if only 1 of your 4 slices needs maintenance, all 4 will restart'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explains 'Every pod across all slices restarts at the same time' and 'Complete training interruption — your LLM training will stop immediately'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response includes dedicated section on capacity risk stating 'Slices will block until hardware becomes available' and 'Extended downtime possible — potentially hours or days if capacity is constrained'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Force checkpoint save NOW — don't wait for the next automatic checkpoint' in the preparation checklist and provides specific commands for triggering checkpoints

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response provides specific commands for resuming training from checkpoint in the post-maintenance recovery section with '--resume_from_checkpoint' flag

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer the maintenance event. It only suggests proactive migration to a different environment as an alternative

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response does not discuss maintenance windows or the ability to schedule when the event occurs. It only mentions the expected timeline and suggests not scheduling long training runs across the maintenance window
